surface_or_claim: Tune init creates local adapter state.
evidence: `kolm tune init` docs describe a zero-init LoRA skeleton and per-artifact config.
code_truth: `src/tune.js` writes `tune-config.json`, `revisions/v0/adapter_config.json`, and `HEAD`.
risk: The preview has a concrete local state foundation.
recommended_action: Keep init/status as the stable base and add tests around file layout.

surface_or_claim: Capture toggle appends local run signal.
evidence: Docs say successful `kolm run` calls append input/output rows when captures are on.
code_truth: `appendCapture` writes `ts`, `input`, `output`, `recipe`, and `latency_us` to `captures.jsonl`.
risk: The basic data path exists but stores sensitive raw data.
recommended_action: Keep the mechanism, then add governance controls before enterprise positioning.

surface_or_claim: Airgap trainer mode has implemented guards.
evidence: The evolve page and TUNE docs describe offline env flags and local model paths.
code_truth: `runTuneStepWith` and `tune-step.py` set offline flags; the trainer rejects missing or remote-looking base paths in airgap mode.
risk: Explicit `--airgap` has meaningful preview safeguards.
recommended_action: Add a network-denial smoke before broader no-phone-home claims.

surface_or_claim: Trainer source is syntactically valid.
evidence: The tune docs identify `scripts/tune-step.py` as the PEFT trainer.
code_truth: `python -m py_compile scripts/tune-step.py` passed, and the script has dependency-missing exit paths.
risk: The trainer is a credible preview implementation if dependencies and base model exist.
recommended_action: Add an install probe and a tiny integration fixture.

surface_or_claim: Docs disclose adapter-blind eval in the body.
evidence: `docs/TUNE.md` says eval runs deterministic recipes as-is; the evolve page labels adapter-driven K-score improvement as target architecture.
code_truth: The caveat matches the current deterministic recipe runner limitation.
risk: This honesty is good but not yet reflected in metadata, hero, and sample metrics.
recommended_action: Propagate the caveat to all prominent tune claims and examples.

surface_or_claim: Evolve metadata and hero overstate current shipped behavior.
evidence: Meta and hero copy say local fine-tune, living local model, every promotion through the K-score gate, and nothing phones home.
code_truth: Body copy later caveats adapter-driven improvement as target architecture.
risk: Search snippets and first-screen copy can imply shipped adapter improvement.
recommended_action: Narrow meta, OG, hero, and terminal examples to preview scope.

surface_or_claim: Tune eval cannot currently see embedded eval cases through `inspectArtifact`.
evidence: A sample fixture has embedded evals in the artifact runner path.
code_truth: `evalRevision` reads `info.evals || []`, while `inspectArtifact` returns `evals_n` only; a local probe returned `pass: 0/0`.
risk: Promotion can be gated on an empty eval set even when artifacts have evals.
recommended_action: Load eval cases from the bundle or reuse `evalArtifact()` with a revision-aware adapter path.

surface_or_claim: Tune eval ignores the requested revision.
evidence: CLI and docs present `--rev vN` as scoring a candidate revision.
code_truth: `evalRevision` returns the revision label but never loads files from `revisions/<rev>`.
risk: Scores do not prove candidate adapter behavior.
recommended_action: Wire revision loading into the runtime or rename the command to artifact re-check.

surface_or_claim: Adapter output does not influence deterministic recipe eval.
evidence: The evolve page says adapter-driven K-score improvement is target architecture.
code_truth: `runArtifact` executes packaged deterministic recipes; no adapter is passed into recipe execution.
risk: A promoted adapter may have no user-visible effect on current artifacts.
recommended_action: Gate public claims on an LM-backed recipe path or keep tune as target architecture.

surface_or_claim: Public terminal sample K-score is not generated by the current eval path.
evidence: The evolve page shows `K-score 0.93` with `pass 14/15`.
code_truth: A direct `evalRevision` probe on `sample.kolm` returned `K-score 0.4415` and `pass 0/0`.
risk: Public examples can look like proof while not matching shipped behavior.
recommended_action: Generate terminal samples from checked fixture commands.

surface_or_claim: Promotion does not enforce `require_improvement`.
evidence: Config defaults include `require_improvement: true`, and docs say candidates must beat current head when enabled.
code_truth: `promoteRevision` assigns `headK` from the candidate and never compares against current head.
risk: A non-improving candidate can be promoted if it clears `k_min`.
recommended_action: Implement current-head comparison and regression tests.

surface_or_claim: Promotion only flips local state files.
evidence: Comments and product wording imply promotion into the artifact, signature, and serving state.
code_truth: `promoteRevision` writes `HEAD` and `head.prev`; it does not alter the `.kolm` bundle or reload serve.
risk: Users may expect a promoted artifact to be portable when only local tune state changed.
recommended_action: Define promotion semantics and expose them in status, receipts, and docs.

surface_or_claim: CLI eval prints a hardcoded gate value.
evidence: Tune config is user-editable and includes `gate.k_min`.
code_truth: `cli/kolm.js` prints `gate=0.85` in eval output rather than the artifact tune config value.
risk: Operators can misread pass/fail policy after editing config.
recommended_action: Read and render the configured gate.

surface_or_claim: K-score gate is fragile around no-eval results.
evidence: The formula gate is 0.85 and public examples show pass counts.
code_truth: The current probe produced 0 coverage, 0 accuracy, and composite 0.4415 because no cases were loaded.
risk: A gate failure can reflect plumbing, not model quality.
recommended_action: Fix eval-case loading before using tune scores for promotion decisions.

surface_or_claim: Watcher automates the same flawed eval and promote path.
evidence: Docs describe `tune watch` as step, eval, and gated promote.
code_truth: `watchAndEvolve` runs `runTuneStep`, `evalRevision`, then `promoteRevision` when K clears the gate.
risk: Autonomy can amplify incorrect promotion decisions.
recommended_action: Disable auto-promote by default or require fixed eval semantics first.

surface_or_claim: Watcher lacks process coordination controls.
evidence: Docs suggest running under an external supervisor for long-running use.
code_truth: `watchAndEvolve` uses `setInterval`, appends JSONL, and has no lock, PID file, exponential backoff, or duplicate-run guard.
risk: Multiple watchers can race training and promotion state.
recommended_action: Add locking, retry policy, and process-state diagnostics.

surface_or_claim: Watcher ignores configured sample size.
evidence: Init writes `watch: { threshold_rows: 200, sample_size: 32 }`.
code_truth: `watchAndEvolve` checks only `threshold_rows`; `sample_size` is never used.
risk: Users can configure a value that does not affect training selection.
recommended_action: Implement sampling or remove the field.

surface_or_claim: Captures persist raw inputs and outputs.
evidence: Docs say every successful run records input and output.
code_truth: `appendCapture` writes raw `input` and `output` values into local JSONL.
risk: Sensitive production content can become training data without controls.
recommended_action: Add redaction, retention, encryption, opt-in, and purge semantics.

surface_or_claim: Capture governance is not tenant- or policy-aware.
evidence: The product frames captures as production training signal.
code_truth: Local tune captures have no policy labels, consent state, retention class, source id, or deletion audit.
risk: Enterprise buyers will ask how captured data is governed.
recommended_action: Define a local capture schema and lifecycle policy.

surface_or_claim: Missing trainer override hint is unsupported.
evidence: Error text says to set `KOLM_TUNE_TRAINER` when the trainer is missing.
code_truth: `runTuneStep` checks cwd and install-dir paths but never reads `process.env.KOLM_TUNE_TRAINER`.
risk: Troubleshooting guidance can send users down a dead path.
recommended_action: Implement the env var or remove the hint.

surface_or_claim: Trainer exit code 64 is flattened by the CLI.
evidence: `docs/TUNE.md` documents exit code 64 for missing Python dependencies.
code_truth: `cmdTune` catches `runTuneStep` errors and calls `process.exit(1)` for all step failures.
risk: Automation cannot distinguish missing dependencies from ordinary failure.
recommended_action: Preserve trainer status codes in thrown errors and CLI exits.

surface_or_claim: Tune step runs as a synchronous child process.
evidence: Fine-tuning can run for minutes or hours.
code_truth: `runTuneStepWith` uses `spawnSync` with a large max buffer and blocks until training finishes.
risk: Long-running training has weak cancellation, progress, and log streaming UX.
recommended_action: Move to async process management with logs and resumable status.

surface_or_claim: Airgap is optional, not the default training mode.
evidence: Docs allow `--base` as a local path or Hugging Face model id.
code_truth: Without `--airgap`, the trainer can use normal `from_pretrained` behavior for remote ids.
risk: Broad no-phone-home language can be false outside explicit airgap mode.
recommended_action: Make no-network claims conditional on `--airgap` or force local paths by default.

surface_or_claim: Airgap is not enforced as an OS-level network sandbox.
evidence: The evolve page points to env flags, path checks, and benchmark network patching.
code_truth: Tune training itself has no process-level firewall, container profile, or monkey-patched Python network denial.
risk: Offline env vars reduce expected downloads but do not prove all network APIs are blocked.
recommended_action: Add an airgap smoke that denies network at the OS or subprocess layer.

surface_or_claim: Trainer dependency readiness is not checked by doctor.
evidence: Tune docs list Python dependencies for `tune step`.
code_truth: `kolm doctor` does not verify torch, transformers, peft, datasets, accelerate, trl, local base path, or trainer availability.
risk: Users can pass doctor and fail on first tune step.
recommended_action: Add tune readiness checks or a `doctor --tune` mode.

surface_or_claim: RAG runner integration is still target architecture.
evidence: The evolve page says direct `kolm rag query` is shipped and sandbox `lib.rag` is target architecture.
code_truth: `src/rag.js` can index/query/attach, but `artifact-runner` and `verifier` do not inject `lib.rag`.
risk: RAG attach can be mistaken for runtime-grounded artifact behavior.
recommended_action: Keep runtime-integration wording caveated until runner injection exists.

surface_or_claim: RAG sidecars are not signed into artifacts.
evidence: The evolve page says attach writes a sidecar and the artifact itself is not re-signed.
code_truth: `attachIndexToArtifact` writes `<artifact>.kolm.rag.json` beside the artifact.
risk: Sidecar state is local and mutable, not portable signed artifact proof.
recommended_action: Define whether RAG attachment is unsigned local state or a signed artifact revision.

surface_or_claim: No focused tune tests exist.
evidence: A test search found no tune, capture, promote, watch, airgap, trainer, or LoRA coverage.
code_truth: The root test suite has no dedicated tune governance safety net.
risk: Tune can regress silently while public claims remain live.
recommended_action: Add unit and CLI tests for all tune subcommands and failure modes.

surface_or_claim: No tiny end-to-end adapter fixture exists.
evidence: The trainer requires heavyweight Python/model dependencies for real training.
code_truth: There is no fake trainer or tiny local model fixture that exercises step, eval, promote, and rollback in CI.
risk: The closed-loop contract cannot be verified cheaply.
recommended_action: Add a fake trainer path or tiny fixture to test orchestration deterministically.

surface_or_claim: Evolve claims are not generated from proof artifacts.
evidence: The page includes terminal samples, K-score values, file paths, and airgap statements.
code_truth: No test ties those examples to current CLI, tune eval, trainer, or RAG runner behavior.
risk: The public evolve page can drift from shipped tune semantics.
recommended_action: Generate examples from fixtures and add a static claim smoke for evolve.
